title: Queues & Concurrency
description: Named queues, concurrency limits, priorities, and debouncing

blocks.text: Hound supports multiple named queues, each processed independently. Handlers declare which queue they run in, and the processor can prioritize queues or limit concurrency globally or per handler.
Assign handlers to queues with the queue option. Jobs emitted without a queue default to 'default':
Limit simultaneous executions globally via HoundOptions.concurrency or per handler:
Per-handler concurrency uses a semaphore internally, so it works correctly even when multiple Hound workers share the same queue.
Set queuePriority in processor options to give certain queues a head start during polling. Lower numbers are processed first:
You can also set per-job priority at emit time with the priority option. Lower values are claimed first within a queue.
The debounce option collapses multiple rapid emits of the same event into a single execution. Useful for search-index updates or user activity bursts:
Debouncing uses the job ID as the dedup key. Only one pending job per event name will be enqueued during the window.
Queues can be paused via the management API. A paused queue stops the processor from claiming new jobs until resumed:
